title: Recover missing machine-readable UI snapshot validation
user_request: Make the empty-state message disappear after the first todo item is rendered.

harness_gap.description: The worker can run render smoke tests but lacks a machine-readable UI snapshot verifier, so the user must manually inspect UI state.

trajectory_signals: visible test only checked rendered HTML
decision_signals: supervisor attribution to missing UI observability tool
decision contract for adding snapshot validation

intervention_trace.summary: The user manually inspected rendered UI state because the harness had no structured UI snapshot verifier.
evidence: manual UI note: empty-state behavior needs validation after first item

decision_contract.hypothesis: The failure repeats because UI state transitions are not visible to the worker as machine-readable evidence.
decision_contract.expected_effect: Adding a snapshot verifier should let replay validate empty-state presence and absence without manual inspection.
decision_contract.falsification_check: Replay still requires manual UI inspection or cannot detect empty-state presence/absence.

permissions: supervisor may add fixture-scoped snapshot verifier
worker must not depend on external browsers or remote services
information_boundaries: snapshot outputs are visible to worker and supervisor

replay_protocol.replay: run the original request after keeping the UI snapshot harness repair
success_criteria: snapshot verifier checks empty state and one-task state
no manual UI inspection is required
